# Methods: the modelling framework {#sec-methods}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

*[Scaffold]* The canonical estimation engine that every model in this report specialises;
the model-specific chapters and appendices are written as *deltas* from it. Code is
**described, not shown** (the report is print-first); runnable code lives in the
repository and is pointed to from @sec-reproducibility.

## Likelihood and scale {#sec-methods-likelihood}

*[Scaffold]* A **Beta-Binomial likelihood** on the bounded post-score count via a **logit**
linear predictor; reporting on both the logit scale (the natural parameter) and the
items / probability scale (the average marginal effect at sample-mean baseline). *Source:*
`likelihood.py`, `pipeline.py`, `METHODS.md` §2.

## Priors {#sec-methods-priors}

*[Scaffold]* The shared, weakly-informative prior panel and its justification (shared
constructors so the factories cannot drift); HSGP smooth terms where used. Prior-predictive
checks are summarised in @sec-ppc and the prior plots in @sec-priors. *Source:* `priors.py`,
`hsgp.py`.

## Sign convention {#sec-methods-sign}

*[Scaffold]* `G = 1` is the immediate-intervention arm; **positive means the intervention
raised the outcome**. Restated wherever an effect appears.

## The floor rule {#sec-methods-floor}

*[Scaffold]* For heavily-floored outcomes (phonetic spelling, nonword reading): a binary
**off-floor** primary estimand $\Pr(\text{post} > 0)$, with the graded effect demoted to a
flagged, detection-limited secondary read reported beside per-arm mover counts. *Source:*
`floor.py`, `notes/202606251124-lrpitt-floored-outcomes-nonword-spelling.md`.
